outcome_text: The characteristics of trustworthy AI are integrated into organizational policies, procedures, and processes.
scoring_text: Skill in integrating trustworthy AI principles — including validity, reliability, safety, security, resilience, accountability, transparency, explainability, interpretability, privacy, and fairness — into organizational governance documents, operational procedures, and business processes.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in integrating trustworthy AI principles — including validity, reliability, safety, security, resilience, accountability, transparency, explainability, interpretability, privacy, and fairness — into organizational governance documents, operational procedures, and business processes.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of responsible AI principles and audit methods, including fairness metric selection, bias test design, explainability documentation standards, and human oversight protocols.
comments: NIST AI RMF GV-1.2 (GOVERN) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-007 is the strongest match (STS: 0.698, cosine: 0.755). Secondary: AG-K-004 (AG, cosine: 0.728), AG-A-003 (AG, cosine: 0.722).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.0/10 (raw 0.698). Shared vocabulary: [ai, explainability, fairness, principles]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [accountability, business, documents, governance, integrating, interpretability] vs WIDAI terms [audit, bias, design, documentation, human, knowledge] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.879 (P=0.874, R=0.884). Balanced P/R — the 30-word competency implication and 22-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.755 > STS 0.698 (gap +0.057). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, explainability, fairness, principles] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 14.3%. Shared tokens: [ai, explainability, fairness, principles]. NIST AI RMF-only: [accountability, business, documents, governance, integrating, interpretability]. WIDAI-only: [audit, bias, design, documentation, human, knowledge].
